(4) Q2 orders repricing at spot if not shipped before end of Q2. (5) Global IT spending $4.96T, AI spending $2.59T (+47% YoY). (6) CPU lead times 8-22 weeks. (7) Power becoming hard ceiling.

Q2 orders repricing at spot if not shipped by end of Q2 creates a hard deadline in 3 weeks (June 30). The Inference Pivot (2/3 of AI compute) means more host CPU/memory per GPU, accelerating DDR5 demand.

escalatedRisks.source: TechTimes / Korea Herald / The Elec June 8-9, 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung DS Vice Chairman Jun met Nvidia CEO Huang at Shilla Hotel Seoul. Discussion covered HBM4E volume supply (Samsung shipped 12-layer HBM4E samples May 29), HBM5 cooperation, and Samsung Foundry manufacturing Nvidia's Groq LP40 inference chip. Three-way competition intensifies HBM wafer allocation and further squeezes DDR5.

escalatedRisks.risk: 🔴🆕🔴 Japan WF6 (tungsten hexafluoride) permanent production halt July 1, 2026 — structural semiconductor supply chain crisis (09:46 second pass discovery)
escalatedRisks.status: CRITICAL NEW - June 10, 2026. Showa Denko Kanto + Central Glass (~25% global WF6 capacity) notified Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC that inventory depleted by end of June, production ceases permanently July 1. Cause: China high-purity tungsten powder exports to Japan ceased January 2026 (60-70% of WF6 cost, +325% to +600-700% YoY). WF6 is irreplaceable for CVD tungsten deposition in DRAM, 3D NAND, and logic chip manufacturing. New supplier qualification: 12-18 months.
escalatedRisks.detail: This is potentially the most significant supply chain disruption in semiconductor history. WF6 is a critical precursor gas for depositing tungsten metal layers in every advanced chip — DRAM bit lines, NAND word lines, and logic interconnects all depend on WF6. With ~25% of global capacity permanently gone from July 1, and 12-18 months to qualify new suppliers, this creates a hard supply cap on ALL semiconductor manufacturing. Combined with the existing HBM wafer competition, MLC NAND shutdown, and enterprise SSD shortages, this could be the tipping point for catastrophic supply failure. The only mitigating factor: ~75% of global capacity remains with other producers (Linde/Air Products/SK Materials in Korea/Hyosung/Solvay/Merck). But demand will immediately exceed remaining supply.
